messages.subject: ClickHouse cost trajectory — are we sure about this?
messages.body:
Marcus,

ClickHouse Cloud hit $1,100 this month. That's up from $800 when we first provisioned it in November. I know we said it would grow with event volume but I want to make sure we have a ceiling in mind.

At 10% month-over-month growth, we're at $2,000/mo by September. You mentioned a $2K threshold before we evaluate self-hosting. Are we still using that number? Because at current growth we'll be there in 7 months.

I'm not saying we made the wrong call — the performance gains are real. I just want to make sure we're tracking this and not getting surprised.

— J

messages.subject: Re: ClickHouse cost trajectory — are we sure about this?
messages.body:
Fair point. $2K threshold stands — we evaluate self-hosting if we consistently exceed $2K for two consecutive months. At that point the EC2 math starts to work again.

To your September estimate: I think you're modeling linear growth but event volume will grow faster than that as we add customers. That said, I want a proper cost model. Can you build a simple spreadsheet — projected ClickHouse cost at 10%, 20%, 30% MoM event growth? I want to share it with David Kim — he has experience with ClickHouse at scale and might have useful input on the self-hosting threshold.

-M

messages.subject: Fwd: ClickHouse cost trajectory — thoughts?
messages.body:
David,

Forwarding a technical thread from our team — curious if you have any input based on what you've seen at scale.

Quick context: we migrated from self-hosted TimescaleDB to ClickHouse Cloud in December. Write performance is dramatically better (p99 18ms at 52M events/day vs 340ms on TimescaleDB). The tradeoff is cost — we're at $1,100/mo now and projecting continued growth as we add customers.

My instinct is that the managed overhead savings justify ClickHouse Cloud up to ~$2K/mo, at which point we evaluate a self-hosted cluster. Have you seen other companies navigate this tradeoff well?

[ignore Jordan's tone in the thread below — he's just being Jordan, he's actually right to flag it, I just want to make sure we're thinking about it clearly rather than reacting to a single month's number]

— Marcus

--- Forwarded thread: ClickHouse cost trajectory ---

[Jordan's message and Marcus's reply are included below this point in the forward]
messages.internal_note: EDGE CASE: Marcus forwarded an internal thread to an external investor. He adds 'ignore Jordan's tone' — framing Jordan's legitimate concern as personality rather than substance. David Kim now has visibility into the cost discussion and Jordan's concerns.

messages.subject: Re: Fwd: ClickHouse cost trajectory — thoughts?
messages.body:
Marcus,

Timely question. Short answer: your $2K threshold is reasonable but I'd push it to $2.5K before triggering a self-hosting evaluation — the engineering time to set up and maintain a ClickHouse cluster will cost you more than $500/mo in opportunity cost at your stage.

Longer answer: the companies I've seen manage this well keep a running cost-per-million-events metric rather than watching total cost. As long as cost/event is flat or declining (which it should be as you get volume discounts), growing absolute cost is actually a good sign. Have Jordan track that instead of the raw dollar figure.

Happy to jump on a call if useful.

— David
